summary: Russia intensified its long‑range strike campaign, launching over 50 missiles and up to 700 drones against Kyiv and other major centers, while reportedly moving a Bastion coastal missile complex to the Kursk region, raising the threat of Oniks and Zircon strikes. Ukraine reported significant civilian casualties in Cherkasy, damage to cultural sites in Kyiv and a successful strike on the Russian Tamannyeftgaz oil terminal in Crimea. Both sides are preparing for possible use of the Oreshnik system, heightening the risk of a strategic escalation.
key_points: Over 50 missiles and 700 drones attacked Kyiv and surrounding districts, hitting more than 40 sites and causing dozens of civilian casualties (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30392).
Ukraine struck the Russian Tamannyeftgaz terminal in Crimea, damaging a key oil‑export facility handling up to 20 Mt annually (Source: @UkraineNow/81420).
Russian intelligence moved a Bastion coastal missile complex (4 launchers) to Kursk, enabling potential Oniks and Zircon strikes from the north (Source: @eRadarrua/93475).
President Zelensky warned of a possible Russian Oreshnik strike, citing intelligence from US and European partners (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
Ukrainian forces reported a massive missile strike on Bila Tserkva (Oreshnik) overnight (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30396).
Cherkasy suffered a Russian UAV attack, injuring 11 people (including 2 children) and causing fires in a multi‑storey residential building (Source: @UkraineNow/81412).
Significant cultural heritage sites in Kyiv, including the National Art Museum and the Chernobyl Museum, were damaged in overnight strikes (Source: @UkraineNow/81421).
Multiple UAV sightings reported over Kyiv reservoir, northern Kyiv region and towards Kharkiv, indicating sustained Russian aerial reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/62641).

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a massive coordinated missile and drone barrage on Kyiv, deploying over 50 missiles and 700 UAVs, while Ukraine hit the Russian Tamannyeftgaz oil terminal in Crimea. Russian forces relocated a Bastion coastal missile complex to Kursk, heightening the threat of Oniks and Zircon strikes, and both sides are preparing for possible use of the Oreshnik system. Civilian casualties rose sharply, with attacks on Cherkasy and cultural sites in Kyiv, underscoring a rapid escalation.

points: Cherkasy was hit by a Russian UAV, injuring 11 civilians (including two children) and igniting fires from the 5th to 9th floors of a residential block; five injured in Fastiv district (Source: @UkraineNow/81412).
Ukrainian forces damaged the Tamannyeftgaz terminal in Crimea, impairing a key oil‑export hub capable of handling up to 20 Mt of oil products per year (Source: @UkraineNow/81420).

The National Art Museum, the Chernobyl Museum, and the historic Kyiv historic reserve suffered structural damage during the latest missile raid, though museum collections remain largely intact (Source: @UkraineNow/81421).
Ukrainian intelligence confirmed a Russian Oreshnik strike on Bila Tserkva overnight, indicating the use of a system originally designed for nuclear payloads (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30396).
Ukrainian emergency services reported two fatalities and 56 injuries in Kyiv after a large‑scale missile attack, with ongoing assessments of building damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81414).
Ukrainian authorities are preparing civil defense measures for a potential Oreshnik strike, urging the public to use shelters and remain vigilant (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
A Russian drone strike in Cherkasy caused a multi‑storey fire, highlighting the continued use of UAVs against civilian infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/81412).
Ukrainian forces claim successful interdiction of Russian missile launches targeting the Kyiv region, reporting several interceptions by air defence (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67193).
Russia relocated a Bastion coastal missile complex with four launchers to the Kursk region, enabling potential Oniks and Zircon strikes from the north (Source: @eRadarrua/93475).
Russian officials hinted at preparing an Oreshnik strike, a system capable of delivering nuclear warheads, as part of a broader strategic intimidation campaign (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
Multiple UAVs were observed over the Kyiv reservoir and moving towards Kharkiv, indicating sustained reconnaissance and possible targeting (Source: @kpszsu/62641).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb releases on Kharkiv and Donetsk regions, intensifying the air‑ground campaign (Source: @kpszsu/62619).
Russian forces launched cruise missiles from the south and central Kyiv region towards the capital, increasing pressure on Ukrainian air defences (Source: @kpszsu/62622).
A Russian missile strike on Bila Tserkva employed the Oreshnik system, marking a rare use of this long‑range weapon (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30396).
Russian air defence reported intercepting a large wave of Ukrainian missiles targeting oil‑gas infrastructure in Kharkiv and Poltava, though some damage persisted (Source: @UkraineNow/81405).
Russian propaganda channels warned of “terrorist” attacks on Ukrainian city centers, framing missile strikes as political intimidation (Source: @kpszsu/62636).
Russian guided bombs were dropped on Kharkiv and Donetsk, causing infrastructure damage and civilian casualties (Source: @kpszsu/62619).
Ukrainian forces reported cruise missile launches from the south and centre of Kyiv oblast heading toward Kyiv, indicating a threat axis from the east (Source: @kpszsu/62622).
Russian UAV activity was noted over the Donetsk region, supporting reconnaissance for upcoming ground operations (Source: @kpszsu/62624).
A large‑scale missile barrage struck the eastern city of Bila Tserkva, employing the Oreshnik system (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30396).
Ukrainian air defences intercepted multiple cruise missiles targeting the eastern industrial hub of Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/62653).
Russian forces reported increased artillery fire on the front line near Luhansk, with shelling intensifying after the Kyiv attacks (Source: @tass_agency/377328).
Ukrainian intelligence noted movement of Russian missile units towards the eastern border, potentially to support a new offensive (Source: @eRadarrua/93489).
Heavy fighting continued around the town of Romny as Russian UAVs approached from the south‑west of Sumy (Source: @kpszsu/62636).
UAVs were tracked over the Kyiv water reservoir, heading south toward the capital, suggesting a northern approach vector (Source: @kpszsu/62641).
Russian cruise missiles launched from the north of Poltava headed toward Cherkasy, indicating a northern strike corridor (Source: @kpszsu/62599).
UAV activity observed over the northern Kyiv region, with some units passing near the village of Vysokhorod (Source: @kpszsu/62641).
Russian forces reportedly moved a Bastion missile complex to Kursk, extending their long‑range strike capability into northern Ukraine (Source: @eRadarrua/93475).
Ukrainian air defences reported intercepting several missiles over the northern oblasts of Chernihiv and Sumy during the large Kyiv raid (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67208).
Russian reconnaissance drones were spotted near the border of Bryansk, monitoring Ukrainian troop movements in the north (Source: @kpszsu/62658).
A Russian missile strike damaged the National Art Museum in Kyiv, located in the northern part of the city centre (Source: @UkraineNow/81421).
Ukrainian forces reported successful downing of multiple Russian UAVs over the northern Kyiv suburbs during the recent barrage (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67193).
Ukrainian forces hit the Tamannyeftgaz oil terminal in Crimea, damaging a key export facility handling up to 20 Mt of oil annually (Source: @UkraineNow/81420).
Russian missile strikes targeted oil‑gas infrastructure in Kharkiv and Poltava, causing severe equipment damage and large fires (Source: @UkraineNow/81405).
Ukrainian reports indicated a Russian UAV attack on the city of Cherkasy, causing civilian injuries and building fires (Source: @UkraineNow/81412).
Russian forces launched cruise missiles from the south toward Kyiv, increasing pressure on the capital from the southern axis (Source: @kpszsu/62622).
Ukrainian air defences intercepted a wave of missiles over the southern oblasts of Mykolaiv and Odesa, though some struck civilian areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30392).
Russian artillery fire intensified in the southern Donetsk region, targeting supply lines near Mariupol (Source: @kpszsu/62624).
Ukrainian intelligence noted movement of Russian missile units towards the Black Sea coast, possibly to support naval operations (Source: @eRadarrua/93489).
A Russian drone strike in the Kherson region caused minor damage to a grain storage facility, highlighting continued pressure on southern logistics (Source: @kpszsu/62636).
Over 50 missiles and up to 700 UAVs were launched against Kyiv in a single coordinated attack, overwhelming air‑defence systems (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30392).
Ukrainian air defences reported multiple successful interceptions of Russian cruise missiles over Kyiv and surrounding oblasts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67193).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided bombs on Kharkiv and Donetsk, indicating a shift to precision strike tactics (Source: @kpszsu/62619).
Ukrainian forces detected UAVs over the Kyiv reservoir and northern Kyiv region, suggesting sustained Russian aerial reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/62641).
Russian Oreshnik system was reportedly used in a strike on Bila Tserkva, marking a rare deployment of this long‑range weapon (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30396).
Ukrainian air‑defence units engaged a large wave of missiles targeting oil‑gas facilities in Kharkiv and Poltava, with mixed success (Source: @UkraineNow/81405).
Russian UAVs were observed moving from the south‑west of Sumy toward Romny, indicating a potential aerial support role for ground operations (Source: @kpszsu/62636).
Ukrainian radar reported a high‑altitude missile trajectory entering Kyiv airspace via the Glavakha corridor, later intercepted (Source: @eRadarrua/93447).

key_developments: Russia launched a coordinated barrage of over 50 missiles and up to 700 drones against Kyiv, striking more than 40 locations and causing dozens of civilian casualties (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30392).
Ukrainian forces successfully struck the Tamannyeftgaz oil terminal in Crimea, damaging a key export hub handling up to 20 Mt of oil annually (Source: @UkraineNow/81420).
Russian intelligence moved a Bastion coastal missile complex (four launchers) to Kursk, enabling potential Oniks and Zircon strikes from the north (Source: @eRadarrua/93475).
President Zelensky warned of a possible Russian Oreshnik strike, citing intelligence from US and European partners (Source: @UkraineNow/81406).
Ukrainian authorities reported severe damage to cultural heritage sites in Kyiv, including the National Art Museum and the Chernobyl Museum, after the missile raid (Source: @UkraineNow/81421).
Russian guided bomb attacks hit Kharkiv and Donetsk, intensifying the air‑ground campaign in the eastern theater (Source: @kpszsu/62619).
Cherkasy suffered a Russian UAV attack, injuring 11 civilians (including two children) and igniting a multi‑storey residential fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81412).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are expanding their long‑range strike capabilities by redeploying the Bastion missile complex to Kursk, which, combined with the potential use of Oniks, Zircon and Oreshnik systems, raises the risk of strategic attacks on deep Ukrainian targets. The massive missile‑drone barrage on Kyiv demonstrates Russia's intent to overwhelm air defences and inflict civilian terror, while Ukraine's successful strike on the Tamannyeftgaz terminal shows its growing ability to hit high‑value Russian assets. Continued UAV reconnaissance over Kyiv and the northern front suggests preparation for further precision strikes, increasing operational volatility across all fronts.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine has accelerated civil defence measures, urging shelter use and reinforcing air‑defence deployments around Kyiv and key industrial zones. International partners, including the United States, have warned of an imminent large‑scale Russian air attack, prompting heightened alert levels across NATO allies. EU officials have condemned the attacks as terror tactics and are coordinating diplomatic pressure on Moscow, while Ukrainian officials are seeking additional air‑defence aid to counter the intensified missile threat.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source monitoring indicates a surge in Russian UAV activity over the Kyiv reservoir and northern suburbs, likely supporting targeting for upcoming missile launches. The relocation of the Bastion complex to Kursk aligns with intelligence on increased Russian focus on northern strike corridors toward Cherkasy and Poltava. Ukrainian damage assessments of cultural sites suggest that Russia is targeting symbols of national identity to undermine morale, while the successful Ukrainian strike on the Crimean oil terminal may prompt retaliatory escalation in the Black Sea region.
